questions.question: The title given to Qutb-ud-din Aibak meaning 'giver of lakhs'.
The mosque started by Qutb-ud-din Aibak in Delhi.
The Sufi saint after whom the Qutub Minar was named.
The group of forty faithful slaves maintained by Iltutmish.
The historian who is the primary source of information about Raziya Sultan.
The practice introduced by Balban where people had to kneel and touch the ground before the Sultan.
The military department created by Balban.
The system of branding horses introduced by Ala-ud-din Khalji.
The court poet during Ala-ud-din Khalji's reign.
The gateway near Qutub Minar considered the best creation of Ala-ud-din Khalji.
The fortified city founded by Ghiyas-ud-din Tughlaq.
The welfare department established by Firoz Shah Tughlaq for the poor.
The title taken by Khizr Khan, founder of the Sayyid Dynasty.
The real name of Sikandar Lodi.
The governor of Punjab who invited Babur to invade India.
